meta.title: ArkyHub — One workspace for every architecture project
meta.description: ArkyHub centralizes plans, BIM models, and virtual tours with version control and role-based access. Replace the patchwork of email, Drive, and WhatsApp.

hero.subhead: ArkyHub is the workspace that centralizes every plan, BIM model, and virtual tour — with version control and the right access for every stakeholder.

mockup.lastUpdated: Last updated · 2h ago by María L.

problem.orderSubhead: One workspace. Every document. Always current.

floorPlans.title: Floor Plans
floorPlans.description: Every plan revision, versioned and searchable. No more v12_final_FINAL.pdf in a Drive folder nobody opens.

bimModels.title: BIM Models
bimModels.description: RVT and IFC in a browser-native viewer. Share the model with consultants and clients without handing out Revit licenses.

virtualTour.title: Virtual Tour
virtualTour.description: 360° walkthroughs stitched from on-site panoramas. Clients step inside the project before the first brick lands.

issueTracker.title: Issue Tracker
issueTracker.description: Punch-list items pinned to the plan coordinates they describe. Close the loop between site and studio in one thread.

stakeholders.title: Stakeholders
stakeholders.description: Role-based access for architects, contractors, consultants, and clients. Everyone sees exactly what they should, nothing more.

guide.headline: We know the moment you stopped trusting your folder structure.
guide.body1: We know what it's like to search for the right version of a drawing while a client waits on the line. We know how it feels to discover the contractor built from a previous version.
guide.body2Pre: ArkyHub is the first product from
guide.body2Post: — a company built to digitalize the AEC sector. It wasn't adapted from construction management software or repurposed from a generic file drive. It was built around how architecture firms actually work.

features.headline: Four things the patchwork can't do.
features.subhead: Each one is a problem we watched architecture firms solve manually, week after week.

items.title: Issues tied to the exact plan version
items.body: When someone flags a problem, it points at the specific drawing version — not floating in a chat thread that gets lost by Friday.

items.title: BIM models, viewable in any browser
items.body: No software install. No license fee. The contractor opens the model on a tablet on-site. The client opens it from their living room.

items.title: Virtual walkthroughs inside the project
items.body: The tour lives next to the plans, not in a separate link buried in an email from three weeks ago.

items.title: Each stakeholder sees only what they need
items.body: Contractors get plans. Clients get the dashboard. Your team manages the rest. One workspace, the right view for everyone.

howItWorks.headline: Three steps. You can be running by lunch.

steps.title: Create your account
steps.body: Set up your firm in minutes. No IT involvement. No procurement cycle.

steps.title: Upload your first project
steps.body: Drop in your plans (DWG or PDF), BIM models, and virtual tours. Versioning starts the moment you upload.

steps.title: Invite your team and stakeholders
steps.body: Each person sees exactly what they need — your team, your contractors, your clients. Nothing more, nothing less.

stats.headline: The numbers that convinced us.
stats.subhead: The industry problem is well-documented. The harder question was whether organized BIM coordination actually moves the needle. We had reason to know it does.

industryStat.text: of rework on U.S. construction sites is caused by poor communication and bad project data.

stats.founderIntro: Then our founder joined a Madrid real estate development firm as BIM Manager. What happened over the next 27 months:

closing.highlight: In Q1 2026 alone, managed m² already exceeded the entire year of 2025.
closing.bold: ArkyHub is the tool we wished existed during those 27 months.

stats.attribution: — Internal data, founder's prior firm. Identifying details omitted by request.

pricing.headline: Start free. Scale when you're ready.
pricing.subhead: One project is free forever — genuinely, not a trial. When your practice outgrows one, the jump is simple.
pricing.smallPrint: All plans include unlimited stakeholders per project, no install, and browser-based BIM/tour viewers. Pricing may evolve before general availability — early adopters keep their rate.

tiers.name: Solo
tiers.tagline: For the architect testing the workflow.

tiers.name: Studio
tiers.tagline: For the practice running multiple projects in parallel.

tiers.name: Firm
tiers.tagline: For firms with dozens of active projects at once.

midCta.headlinePre: You've seen what it costs to wait.
midCta.headlineAccent: See what it looks like to stop.

faq.headline: What architects ask before signing up.

items.q: Do contractors need to install anything?
items.a: No. ArkyHub runs entirely in the browser — including the BIM viewer. Your contractor opens a link, sees the latest plan. That's it.

items.q: How is this different from Google Drive?
items.a: Drive stores files. ArkyHub knows what they are. Plans get version control. Issues get tied to the exact drawing. BIM and virtual tours render natively. Each stakeholder gets a different view.

items.q: How is it different from Procore or Autodesk Construction Cloud?
items.a: Those are built for contractors and run by contractors. ArkyHub is built for the architect — you control the documentation, you decide what each stakeholder sees.

items.q: What file formats do you support?
items.a: Plans in DWG and PDF. BIM models in IFC and RVT. Virtual tours via standard panoramic formats. If your workflow depends on a specific export pipeline, schedule a demo and we'll confirm the fit.

items.q: Is my client data isolated from my other projects?
items.a: Yes. Each firm — and each project within a firm — is fully separated. Working with multiple clients never means data leaks between them.
We're rolling out access with early-adopter pricing. Schedule a demo and we'll walk you through what fits your firm size.

faq.ctaPre: Have a question we didn't answer?

finalCta.eyebrow: Two versions of your next project
finalCta.headline: The same project. Two different weeks.

comparison.without: You spend Tuesday morning searching for the latest plan.
comparison.with: The latest plan is the only plan anyone can see.

comparison.without: Your contractor calls — they built from a previous version.
comparison.with: Your contractor only ever sees the approved version.

comparison.without: Your client emails: “any update?” You don’t have a fast answer.
comparison.with: Your client opens the dashboard. They have their answer before they email.

comparison.without: Issues live in a WhatsApp group nobody scrolls back through.
comparison.with: Every issue points to the exact drawing version it came from.

finalCta.closingPre: One workspace. Every document. Every stakeholder.
